title: Story schema and CRUD with two-tier status model

story.i_want_to: create, read, update, list, and delete stories within an epic, where each story has a number, title, description, acceptance_criteria (jsonb), estimated_hours, agent_status enum, verified_status enum, assignment fields, and metadata
story.so_that: the atomic units of work are tracked with the two-tier trust model where agent-reported status and independently verified status are separate fields

rationale: Stories are the fundamental work unit in loopctl. The two-tier status model (agent_status + verified_status) is the core innovation: implementing agents set agent_status, while orchestrators independently set verified_status. This structural separation makes it impossible for an agent to self-verify. Acceptance criteria stored as jsonb enable structured verification checks. The assignment fields (assigned_agent_id, assigned_at) track which agent is working on what.

acceptance_criteria.description: A Loopctl.WorkBreakdown.Story schema exists with all fields from PRD section 5.1: id, tenant_id, epic_id, number (string), title, description, acceptance_criteria (jsonb), estimated_hours (decimal), agent_status (enum), verified_status (enum), assigned_agent_id, assigned_at, reported_done_at, verified_at, rejected_at, rejection_reason, metadata (jsonb), inserted_at, updated_at
agent_status enum has values: pending, assigned, implementing, reported_done. Default: :pending
verified_status enum has values: unverified, verified, rejected. Default: :unverified
Story number is unique within an epic (composite unique index on [epic_id, number]); number is a string (e.g., '2.1', '2.2')
POST /api/v1/epics/:epic_id/stories (role: user) creates a story; requires number and title, optional description, acceptance_criteria, estimated_hours, metadata
GET /api/v1/epics/:epic_id/stories (role: agent+) lists all stories in an epic, with optional filtering by agent_status and verified_status query params
GET /api/v1/stories/:id (role: agent+) returns a single story with dependencies, latest artifact reports, and latest verification result preloaded

PATCH /api/v1/stories/:id (role: user) updates story metadata fields (title, description, acceptance_criteria, estimated_hours, metadata). Cannot update agent_status or verified_status via this endpoint — dedicated status endpoints handle those.
All queries are scoped to the current tenant via both application-level WHERE clause and RLS policy. RLS is enabled on the stories table.
Every mutation (create, update, delete) MUST be recorded in the audit log via Ecto.Multi in the same transaction as the entity change.

test_cases.name: Create story with valid attributes
preconditions: fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
expected_results: Response status is 201
Response body has story with agent_status = "pending" and verified_status = "unverified"
acceptance_criteria is stored as provided jsonb
Story exists in database with correct tenant_id and epic_id

test_cases.name: Reject duplicate story number within epic
preconditions: fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
expected_results: Response status is 409 or 422 with error on number

test_cases.name: List stories with status filtering
preconditions: fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
expected_results: Only stories with agent_status = pending are returned
The implementing story is not in the response

test_cases.name: PATCH cannot update agent_status or verified_status
preconditions: fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id, agent_status: :pending}) as story
expected_results: Response status is 200
Title is updated to "Updated Title"
agent_status remains :pending (status field is ignored/filtered)
verified_status remains :unverified (status field is ignored/filtered)

test_cases.name: Get story includes dependencies and artifacts
preconditions: fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id}) as story
expected_results: Response includes story with all fields
Response includes dependencies array (empty if none)
Response includes artifacts array (empty if none)
Response includes latest_verification (null if none)

test_cases.name: Story queries are tenant-scoped via RLS
preconditions: fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant_b.id, project_id: project_b.id}) as epic_b
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant_b.id, epic_id: epic_b.id}) as other_story
expected_results: Response status is 404 (cannot access another tenant's story)

test_cases.name: Story defaults are set correctly
steps: Create a Story changeset with only required fields (number, title, tenant_id, epic_id)
expected_results: agent_status defaults to :pending
verified_status defaults to :unverified
metadata defaults to %{}
acceptance_criteria defaults to [] or nil
assigned_agent_id is nil

test_cases.name: Delete story removes it from database
preconditions: fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id}) as story
expected_results: Response status is 200 or 204
Story no longer exists in the database
GET /api/v1/stories/<story.id> returns 404

test_cases.name: Mutation creates audit log entry
preconditions: fixture(:epic, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id}) as epic
steps: Perform the mutation via API endpoint
expected_results: Audit log entry created with correct entity_type, entity_id, action, and actor
Audit entry is in the same transaction (no orphaned entity without audit)

technical_notes: Story number is a STRING, not an integer — allows formats like '2.1', '2.2', '10.1'
The PATCH endpoint must use a separate changeset function (update_changeset) that explicitly excludes agent_status and verified_status from cast/3
acceptance_criteria is stored as jsonb — no validation on its internal structure beyond being valid JSON
The show endpoint preloads are: dependencies (story_dependencies), artifact_reports (latest N), verification_results (latest)
estimated_hours uses Decimal type for precision (e.g., 0.5 hours)
Consider adding a max_stories_per_project limit check from tenant settings
Schema module MUST use `use Loopctl.Schema` (provides `@primary_key {:id, :binary_id, autogenerate: true}`, `@foreign_key_type :binary_id`, `@timestamps_opts [type: :utc_datetime_usec]`)
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
